# Bellwether

> Prediction market infrastructure for research, journalism, and intelligent systems. Built at Stanford GSB and the Hoover Institution.

Bellwether aggregates political prediction markets from Polymarket and Kalshi into a single, standardized dataset with live VWAP pricing, cross-platform canonical tickers, and editorial reportability scores. It is a research institution — it does not operate an exchange.

Website: https://bellwethermetrics.com
API Docs: https://bellwethermetrics.com/docs.html
MCP Server: npx bellwether-mcp
Contact: paschal@stanford.edu

## Team

- Andrew B. Hall — Principal Investigator. Davies Family Professor of Political Economy, Stanford GSB. Senior Fellow, Hoover Institution.
- Elliot Paschal — Research Fellow. Stanford Graduate School of Business.

Supported by the Hoover Institution and the Stanford Graduate School of Business.

## Products

### Tape API
REST API returning live prediction market data. Every market is scored for manipulation resistance, matched across platforms via canonical tickers, and tagged with a reportability label. Cached for 30 seconds.

Open access. No authentication required. Academic and research use is free. Email paschal@stanford.edu for questions or support.

Base URL: https://api.bellwethermetrics.com/api

### Market Blueprints
Open, immutable contract specifications for prediction markets. A blueprint defines the event, resolution source, resolution criteria, edge cases, fallback sources, and settlement rules — all specified before trading begins. Any platform can list a blueprint. All resolve identically.

### Research Dashboard
Interactive analysis of prediction market accuracy, calibration, liquidity, and partisan bias across resolved markets. Includes Brier score convergence, calibration curves (quantile binning), liquidity-accuracy analysis, and platform comparison.

### Embeds
Drop a live Bellwether market card into any article. Provide a BWR ticker to generate an iframe snippet for your CMS.

Embed URL format: https://bellwethermetrics.com/embed.html?ticker={TICKER}

Example:
<iframe src="https://bellwethermetrics.com/embed.html?ticker=BWR-DEM-CONTROL-HOUSE-CERTIFIED-ANY-FEB2027" width="100%" height="200" frameborder="0"></iframe>

### MCP Server
Model Context Protocol server giving AI agents direct access to live market prices, search, and data quality assessments. Compatible with Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, and any MCP-compatible client. Published on npm and the official MCP registry.

Install: npx bellwether-mcp

Claude Desktop configuration (add to claude_desktop_config.json):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "bellwether": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["bellwether-mcp"]
    }
  }
}

Claude Code: claude mcp add bellwether -- npx bellwether-mcp

## API Endpoints

### GET /api/metrics/event/{slug}
Get single event with live data. Returns live price, VWAP, robustness metrics, and reportability for an event resolved via the market index. Data is fetched from native Polymarket and Kalshi APIs in real time (cached for 30 seconds).

Parameters:
- slug (required, string): Bellwether event slug, e.g. bwr-fitzgerald-win-pres-ie

Response fields:
- bellwether_price (float): Volume-weighted price (VWAP). Null if insufficient trade data.
- price_tier (int): Data quality tier: 1 (6h VWAP, 10+ trades), 2 (12-24h VWAP), 3 (stale or no data).
- price_label (string): Human-readable description of the price source, e.g. "6h VWAP across platforms".
- price_source (string): Machine-readable: "6h_vwap", "12h_vwap", "24h_vwap", "stale_vwap", or "no_data".
- robustness (object): Contains cost_to_move_5c, cost_to_move_up_5c, cost_to_move_down_5c, reportability, raw_reportability, and weakest_platform.
- vwap_details (object): Contains window_hours, trade_count, and total_volume.
- orderbook_midpoint (float): Best bid/ask midpoint from current order book.
- orderbook_summary (object): Contains bid_levels, ask_levels, best_bid, best_ask, top_5_bids, top_5_asks. For cross-platform markets, each entry includes a "platform" field ("polymarket" or "kalshi").
- fetched_at (datetime): When upstream data was last fetched. Cached for 30 seconds.

### GET /api/markets/{ticker}
Get market by BWR ticker. Returns live price, VWAP, robustness metrics, reportability, and platform availability for a market identified by its canonical Bellwether ticker. This is the primary way to look up a specific market.

Parameters:
- ticker (required, string): Bellwether ticker, e.g. BWR-DEM-WIN-SENATE_TX-CERTIFIED-ANY-2026

Response fields:
- ticker (string): Canonical BWR ticker.
- slug (string): URL-friendly identifier.
- title (string): Human-readable market title.
- category (string): Political category, e.g. "ELECTORAL".
- country (string): ISO country code, e.g. "us".
- platforms (array): Platforms this market trades on, e.g. ["polymarket", "kalshi"].
- volume_usd (float): Total trading volume in USD.
- bellwether_price (float): Volume-weighted price (VWAP). Null if insufficient data.
- price_tier (int): Data quality tier: 1, 2, or 3.
- robustness (object): Contains cost_to_move_5c, cost_to_move_up_5c, cost_to_move_down_5c, reportability, weakest_platform.
- orderbook_summary (object): Contains bid_levels, ask_levels, best_bid, best_ask, top_5_bids, top_5_asks. For cross-platform markets, entries include "platform".
- fetched_at (datetime): When upstream data was last fetched.

### GET /api/metrics/{platform}/{token_id}
Platform market data. For direct access to platform-specific data. Fetches live order book and trade data from the native Polymarket CLOB and Kalshi Elections APIs. Returns tiered VWAP price, midpoint, order book depth, and reportability for a single platform market.

Parameters:
- platform (required, string): "polymarket" or "kalshi"
- token_id (required, string): Polymarket token ID or Kalshi market ticker

Response fields:
- bellwether_price (float): Tiered VWAP price. Null if insufficient trade data.
- price_tier (int): Data quality tier: 1 (6h VWAP), 2 (12-24h VWAP), 3 (stale/no data).
- price_label (string): Human-readable source label, e.g. "6h VWAP".
- price_source (string): Machine-readable: "6h_vwap", "12h_vwap", "24h_vwap", "stale_vwap", or "no_data".
- robustness (object): Contains cost_to_move_5c, cost_to_move_up_5c, cost_to_move_down_5c, reportability, and raw_reportability.
- vwap_details (object): Contains window_hours, trade_count, and total_volume.
- orderbook_midpoint (float): Best bid/ask midpoint from current order book.
- orderbook_summary (object): Contains bid_levels, ask_levels, best_bid, best_ask, top_5_bids, top_5_asks. Each entry has price, size.
- fetched_at (datetime): When upstream data was last fetched. Cached for 30 seconds.

### GET /api/metrics/combined
Cross-platform aggregation. Returns volume-weighted combined data for a matched pair. The combined VWAP is computed across trades from both platforms in the same time window.

Parameters:
- pm_token (string): Polymarket token ID
- k_ticker (string): Kalshi market ticker
- Provide at least one. Provide both for cross-platform aggregation.

Response fields:
- bellwether_price (float): Combined VWAP price across both platforms.
- price_tier (int): Data quality tier: 1, 2, or 3.
- robustness (object): Contains cost_to_move_5c, cost_to_move_up_5c, cost_to_move_down_5c, reportability, raw_reportability, weakest_platform.
- orderbook_midpoint (float): Best bid/ask midpoint from merged order book.
- orderbook_summary (object): Contains bid_levels, ask_levels, best_bid, best_ask, top_5_bids, top_5_asks. Each entry includes a "platform" field ("polymarket" or "kalshi").
- fetched_at (datetime): When upstream data was last fetched.

### GET /api/markets/search
Search active political prediction markets by keyword.

Parameters:
- q (required, string): Search query
- category (optional, string): Filter by political category
- limit (optional, int): Max results, default 10, max 50

Response fields: Array of results with slug, ticker, title, category, volume_usd, is_matched, platforms.

### GET /api/markets/top
Returns the most actively traded prediction markets by volume.

Parameters:
- category (optional, string): Filter by political category
- limit (optional, int): Max results, default 10, max 50

Response fields: Array of results with slug, ticker, title, category, volume_usd, is_matched, platforms.

## Ticker Format

All markets are assigned a canonical Bellwether ticker for cross-platform identification.

Format: BWR-AGENT-ACTION-TARGET-MECHANISM-THRESHOLD-TIMEFRAME

Components:
- AGENT: The actor, party, or institution (DEM, GOP, FED, TRUMP, ZELENSKYY)
- ACTION: What the agent does (WIN, CONTROL, CUT, HIKE, PARDON, LEAVE)
- TARGET: What the action applies to (PRES, SEN_PA, GOV_CA, FFR, HOUSE)
- MECHANISM: How the event resolves (CERTIFIED, SPECIFIC_MEETING, ANY_MEANS)
- THRESHOLD: Numeric threshold or qualifier (ANY, 5BPS, 25BPS, GT_2.0PCT)
- TIMEFRAME: When the event occurs (2026, JAN2026, 2025_Q4, DEC2025)

Matching rule: Two markets on different platforms match if and only if their tickers are identical. Post-processing rules normalize equivalent resolution mechanisms (e.g. "projected" = "certified" for elections, "primary" = "certified" for nominations).

Examples:
- BWR-DEM-CONTROL-HOUSE-CERTIFIED-ANY-2026 — Party control
- BWR-FITZGERALD-WIN-PRES_IE-CERTIFIED-ANY-2025 — Election
- BWR-FED-CUT-FFR-SPECIFIC_MEETING-5BPS-JAN2026 — Monetary policy
- BWR-TRUMP-PARDON-MAXWELL-SIGNED_INTO_LAW-ANY-2026 — Executive action
- BWR-NEWSOM-WIN-DEM_NOMINATION-CERTIFIED-ANY-NOV2028 — Party nomination

## Reportability System

Every market is scored for manipulation resistance based on live order book depth. The cost_to_move_5c field (nested inside robustness) tells you how many dollars it would take to shift the market price by 5 cents — the core metric behind the reportability tiers.

Tiers:
- Reportable: cost_to_move_5c >= $100,000. Safe to cite. Price reflects broad market consensus.
- Caution: $10,000 to $99,999. Cite with context. Note liquidity level.
- Fragile: < $10,000. Do not cite as consensus. Easily manipulated.

Tier adjustments based on data freshness:
- Tier 2 data (12-24h VWAP): Downgrades reportable to caution, caution to fragile.
- Tier 3 data (stale or no trades): Always fragile regardless of depth.

## VWAP Pricing

The Bellwether price is a volume-weighted average price (VWAP): sum(price * size) / sum(size). It weights each transaction by size. For cross-platform markets, transactions from both Polymarket and Kalshi are pooled into a single VWAP.

Tier 1 (best): 6-hour window, 10+ trades required. Price source: "6h_vwap".
Tier 2 (acceptable): 12 or 24-hour window. Price source: "12h_vwap" or "24h_vwap". Reportability downgraded one tier.
Tier 3 (poor): Stale VWAP or no data. Price source: "stale_vwap" or "no_data". bellwether_price is null if insufficient data. Always fragile.

Fallback cascade: Try 6h VWAP with 10+ trades. If insufficient, try 12h then 24h. If still insufficient, return stale or no_data.

## Political Categories

All markets are classified into one of 15 political categories:

- ELECTORAL — Elections and nominations
- MONETARY_POLICY — Central bank and interest rates
- INTERNATIONAL — International affairs
- POLITICAL_SPEECH — Statements and public speech
- MILITARY_SECURITY — Military and security
- APPOINTMENTS — Appointments and nominations
- TIMING_EVENTS — Timing and scheduling
- JUDICIAL — Courts and legal
- PARTY_POLITICS — Party politics
- GOVERNMENT_OPERATIONS — Government operations
- REGULATORY — Regulatory
- LEGISLATIVE — Legislation and policy
- POLLING_APPROVAL — Polls and approval ratings
- STATE_LOCAL — State and local government
- CRISIS_EMERGENCY — Crisis and emergency

## Market Blueprints

Blueprints are open, immutable contract specifications. Each blueprint contains:

- Ticker: Canonical identifier in BWR format.
- Event: Plain-language description eliminating ambiguity.
- Source: External authority for outcome determination. Never the platform itself.
- Criteria: Precise conditions for YES, NO, or N/A resolution.
- Edge Cases: Explicit handling of foreseeable ambiguities before trading begins.
- Fallback: What happens if the primary source is unavailable or disputed.
- Settlement: When and how resolution is evaluated.
- Expiration: Date after which the market resolves NO if the event has not occurred.

Published blueprints include specifications for U.S. military action in Venezuela, Israeli ground incursion into Lebanon, U.S. federal government shutdown, U.S. debt ceiling resolution, comprehensive U.S. federal AI legislation, and party control of the U.S. Senate after 2026 midterms.

Organizations can commission custom blueprints. Process: define the question, Bellwether drafts the spec, organization reviews, spec is published as immutable, any platform can list it.

## MCP Server Tools

The bellwether-mcp package provides five tools for AI agents via MCP (Model Context Protocol).

### search_markets
Search active political prediction markets by topic or keyword.
Parameters: query (required, string), category (optional, string), limit (optional, number, default 10, max 50).
Returns: Array of results with ticker, title, category, volume_usd, is_matched, platforms.

### get_market
Get live VWAP price, data quality tier, reportability, and editorial guidance for a specific market.
Parameters: ticker (required, string, must start with "BWR-").
Returns: ticker, title, bellwether_price, price_label, price_tier, reportability, guidance, cost_to_move_5c, trade_count, fetched_at.

### get_top_markets
Get the most actively traded prediction markets, optionally filtered by category.
Parameters: category (optional, string), limit (optional, number, default 10, max 50).
Returns: Array of results with ticker, title, category, volume_usd, is_matched, platforms.

### get_platform_spread
Compare prices between Kalshi and Polymarket for cross-platform matched markets. Returns spread in cents and direction.
Parameters: ticker (required, string, must start with "BWR-").
Returns: ticker, kalshi_price, polymarket_price, spread_cents, spread_direction, reportability, fetched_at.

### get_reportability
Check whether a market is safe to cite in published content. Returns reportability tier, cost to move price 5 cents, and editorial guidance.
Parameters: ticker (required, string).
Returns: ticker, reportability, cost_to_move_5c, guidance, price_tier, fetched_at.

## Data Glossary

### Master Dataset (political_markets_master.csv)
One row per market outcome. All markets classified into 15 political categories.

Identifiers:
- market_id (string): Unique identifier for the market (platform-specific).
- platform (string): Source platform: "polymarket" or "kalshi".
- pm_condition_id (string): Polymarket condition ID.
- pm_token_id_yes (string): Polymarket YES token ID.
- bellwether_ticker (string): Canonical Bellwether ticker for cross-platform matching.

Classification:
- question (string): Full market question text.
- political_category (string): One of 15 categories (see Political Categories).
- volume_usd (float): Total trading volume in USD.
- is_matched (bool): True if matched with a market on the other platform.

### Prices Dataset (price_histories.csv)
Daily price observations at multiple time horizons before resolution. One row per market per time horizon.

- market_id (string): Links to master table.
- days_before_event (int): Days before resolution (60, 30, 14, 7, 3, 1).
- prediction_price (float): Market price (0-1), interpretable as probability.
- actual_outcome (float): Resolution outcome (1.0 = Yes, 0.0 = No).
- brier_score (float): (prediction - outcome)^2. Scale 0-1, lower is better.

### Liquidity Dataset (liquidity_metrics.csv)
Order book snapshots and derived liquidity metrics per market.

- market_id (string): Links to master table.
- spread_mean (float): Mean bid-ask spread (absolute, 0-1).
- spread_median (float): Median bid-ask spread.
- depth_mean (float): Mean total order book depth (contracts at best bid + ask).
- bid_depth_mean (float): Mean depth on bid side.
- ask_depth_mean (float): Mean depth on ask side.
- depth_imbalance_mean (float): Mean order book imbalance: (bid - ask) / (bid + ask).
- n_snapshots (int): Number of order book snapshots collected.

## Response Format

All API responses are JSON. Timestamps are ISO 8601. Prices are decimal probabilities (0.00 to 1.00). Upstream data is cached for 30 seconds.
